reforms.description: Slovenia has introduced a new funding formula for universities, where 75% of funding is fixed per institution and the remaining 25% is linked to student enrollment and output indicators, such as scientific output and employment prospects of graduates. Additionally, the government has enhanced its vocational training system by linking students with local firms for temporary work experience, and introduced a pilot programme for a dual apprenticeship-training system starting in the 2017-18 school year, aiming to increase practical training to at least 50% of the total educational programme.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reforms aim to improve the quality of education and align it with labor market needs, thereby enhancing employability and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been put into practice as of the 2017-18 school year.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a new funding formula and dual apprenticeship system represents a significant overhaul of the education and training framework.
reforms.source_quote: This could be achieved, for example, through the existing Practical Training with Work programme, which links four-year students with local firms for temporary work experience during term time.

reforms.description: Slovenia adopted the Smart Specialisation Strategy in 2015, which defines priority areas for R&D investments and innovations, aiming to implement a unified innovation policy and monitor its progress.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By focusing R&D investments on priority areas, the strategy aims to enhance innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The strategy has been officially adopted and is in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The strategy represents a significant policy shift towards targeted R&D investment.
reforms.source_quote: In 2015, the Smart Specialisation Strategy was adopted, which defines priority areas for R&D investments and innovations.

reforms.description: In 2016, Slovenia established innovation partnerships to improve collaboration between stakeholders in innovation policy, enhancing the framework for coordination and monitoring.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improved collaboration among stakeholders is expected to enhance the effectiveness of innovation policies.
reforms.status_evidence: The partnerships have been established and are operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the establishment of partnerships is a reform that enhances existing frameworks rather than a complete overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: In 2016, innovation partnerships to improve collaboration between stakeholders of innovation policy were created.

reforms.description: The government has launched an ambitious national broadband plan, Digital Slovenia, aiming to provide fast broadband internet access speeds of at least 100 Mbps to 96% of households by 2020, which is expected to promote digitalisation in the economy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving broadband access is crucial for enhancing digital capabilities and fostering innovation across sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan has been launched but is not yet fully implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: The broadband plan represents a significant investment in national infrastructure aimed at supporting digital transformation.
reforms.source_quote: The government has an ambitious national broadband plan (Digital Slovenia) to provide fast broadband internet access speeds of at least 100 Mbps to 96% of households by 2020.

reforms.description: The Slovene government has increased support for firms through various funding sources, including the SPIRIT Agency and the Slovene Enterprise Fund, aimed at boosting exports and attracting foreign direct investment (FDI).
reforms.component_name: SPIRIT Agency and Enterprise Fund Support
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increased support for firms is expected to enhance their competitiveness and drive economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The support measures have been put into practice.
reforms.importance_rationale: While the support measures are significant, they are part of ongoing efforts rather than a major reform.
reforms.source_quote: There are also a number of governmental supports to firms, which increased in value in the early stages of the crisis.
